name: Maternal minute ventilation at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015 (PMID 27066123) and consensus across multiple secondary obstetric-physiology references: resting minute ventilation rises from ~6.5-7.5 L/min in early pregnancy to ~10-10.5 L/min at term (30-50% increase), driven primarily by tidal-volume expansion and progesterone-mediated central drive. The 10.5 L/min term central is at the upper end of the published range.

extraction.method: triangulated from LoMauro 2015 + multiple obstetric-physiology references

notes: Resting minute ventilation at term; increased ~30-50% above non-pregnant baseline.

name: Pregnancy P50 shift
tier_rationale: Tier B from Kelman 1966 J Appl Physiol: the original digital subroutine for O2-Hb saturation, including temperature and pH shifts. Used as the foundational reference for pregnancy P50 shifts attributable to 2,3-DPG and pH changes.
notes: Rightward shift in maternal oxyhaemoglobin P50 in pregnancy, attributed to elevated 2,3-BPG. Caveats: Reported shift varies by methodology and 2,3-BPG measurement; a 2-4 mmHg range is most commonly cited.

name: Maternal arterial PCO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier A from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015 ERS Breathe review: arterial PaCO2 'plateaus around 32 mmHg' at term, consistent with progesterone-driven hyperventilation. Multiple independent cohorts referenced in the masterclass review.
notes: PaCO2 at term, post-hyperventilation plateau. Updated from the original v0.2 value of 30 mmHg to 32 mmHg to match the value reported in LoMauro & Aliverti 2015. The original 30 was likely an extraction error; the original 'high' bound (32) matched the paper's plateau value.

name: Maternal arterial PO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier A from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015 ERS Breathe review: PaO2 'reaches 101-104 mmHg in the third trimester' as a compensatory rise after PaCO2 falls. Range tightened to match the third-trimester window reported.
notes: PaO2 at term, post-hyperventilation. Range tightened from 98-108 (which spans first- and third-trimester) to 101-105, matching the third-trimester range reported by LoMauro 2015.

name: Maternal tidal volume at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Crapo 1996 Clinical Obstetrics and Gynecology (PMID 8635306) and consensus across the obstetric-physiology literature: resting tidal volume at term rises from a non-pregnant baseline of ~480 mL to ~680 mL, an increase of ~30-40%, accounting for most of the minute-ventilation rise. Replicated across LoMauro 2015 (PMID 27066123) and standard respiratory-physiology texts.

extraction.method: triangulated from Crapo 1996 + LoMauro 2015 + secondary references

name: Functional residual capacity at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015 (PMID 27066123) + Crapo 1996 (PMID 8635306): FRC falls ~20% from a non-pregnant baseline of ~2.5 L to ~2.0 L at term, driven by gravid uterus elevating the diaphragm. The fall begins second trimester and is the source of the reduced apnoeic O2 reserve that complicates rapid-sequence induction in pregnancy.
notes: 20% drop in FRC reduces maternal apnoeic O2 reserve and shortens safe-apnoea time — clinically important for airway management.

name: Resting oxygen consumption at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015: basal O2 consumption rises ~20% above non-pregnant baseline of ~250 mL/min, reflecting the metabolic load of fetus + placenta + maternal cardiovascular and respiratory work. Combined with the ~15% rise in CO2 production, the ventilatory drive shift explains the term hypocapnia (paco2 ~32 mmHg).

name: Maternal non-pregnant tidal volume
tier_rationale: Tier A from LoMauro 2015 review: non-pregnant VT ~450 mL in healthy adult women; well-established.

name: Maternal non-pregnant respiratory rate
tier_rationale: Tier A: non-pregnant adult RR ~12-20/min; LoMauro 2015 review confirms RR is approximately constant through pregnancy.

name: Maternal term respiratory rate
tier_rationale: Tier A from LoMauro 2015: RR is essentially unchanged in pregnancy; the minute-ventilation rise is driven almost entirely by tidal volume.

name: Maternal non-pregnant arterial PO2
tier_rationale: Tier A: non-pregnant PaO2 at sea level ~100 mmHg; Templeton & Kelman 1976 confirms the modest pregnancy rise.

name: Maternal non-pregnant arterial pH
tier_rationale: Tier A: non-pregnant arterial pH 7.40 ± 0.02; baseline against which the mild pregnancy alkalosis (pH ~7.44) is measured.

name: Maternal arterial pH at term
tier_rationale: Tier B: compensated respiratory alkalosis of pregnancy. Multiple longitudinal cohorts (Templeton & Kelman 1976, Lim 1976, Hegewald 2011) converge on ~7.44 at term; ~0.04 pH unit rise above non-pregnant baseline, partially compensated by renal HCO3 excretion.

name: Inspiratory capacity at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Crapo 1996 and LoMauro & Aliverti 2015: inspiratory capacity (IC = TV + IRV) rises modestly through pregnancy by ~5-10% from non-pregnant ~2.5 L to ~2.7 L at term. The rise is attributed to expansion of the rib cage at the expense of the descended diaphragm; it partly compensates for the fall in FRC.
notes: FRC + IC = TLC. Since TLC is approximately unchanged and FRC falls, IC must rise; the magnitudes match the FRC drop already in the dataset.

name: Total lung capacity at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Crapo 1996: total lung capacity is essentially unchanged through pregnancy (typically <5% reduction), reflecting that the diaphragmatic ascent is offset by rib-cage expansion. The preserved TLC against falling FRC means breathing operates from a lower baseline volume — relevant to closing-volume considerations in late pregnancy.

name: Maternal CO2 production at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015: maternal CO2 production (VCO2) rises ~15-20% from a non-pregnant baseline of ~210 mL/min to ~250 mL/min at term, paralleling the rise in O2 consumption (already in the dataset). The roughly matched VO2/VCO2 rises keep the resting respiratory exchange ratio near 0.85.

applicability.population: Healthy singleton pregnancies at rest, third trimester

notes: Combined with VO2 already in the dataset, VCO2/VO2 ≈ 0.85 (resting RER preserved).

name: Single-breath DLCO at term
tier_rationale: Tier C from Crapo 1996: single-breath diffusing capacity for CO (DLCO) shows a small fall through pregnancy (typically a few percent to ~10%) relative to non-pregnant baseline (~28 mL/min/mmHg). The reduction is attributed to modest haemodilution outweighing the rise in pulmonary blood flow. Individual studies disagree on magnitude; Tier C reflects the heterogeneity.

name: Alveolar-arterial O2 gradient at term
tier_rationale: Tier C from Crapo 1996: the A-a O2 gradient widens modestly through pregnancy and especially in the supine position, with term values centred around 10-15 mmHg compared to non-pregnant ~5-10. The widening is attributed to closing-volume encroachment on FRC and modest V/Q mismatch in dependent zones. Substantial inter-individual variation justifies Tier C.

applicability.population: Healthy singleton pregnancies, third trimester, supine or sitting

notes: Used clinically as part of the lower threshold for diagnosing pulmonary embolism in pregnant patients.
